one_time_description: Pandemic response expenses related to COVID-19
economic_notes: First Nation operates Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar. Has 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Inc. (Swift Current Casino Development) and 4.5% interest in IMI Brokerage Limited Partnership. Involved in legal claim regarding Last Mountain Indian Reserve 80A unlawfully surrendered land, expected to receive 11.25% of proceeds upon settlement.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Consolidated entity includes Standing Buffalo Dakota First Nation, CMHC Housing, Standing Buffalo Care Home Inc., Dakota Development Corporation, and Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar. Significant COVID-19 pandemic response expenses of $851,224. Contingent asset related to Last Mountain Indian Reserve 80A land claim (11.25% share). CEBA loan of $30,000 with potential $10,000 forgivable portion. Gain on disposal of tangible capital assets of $18,000 recorded as other income. No remuneration schedule found in provided text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($11,437,798) plus CMHC subsidies/rent/RRAP ($737,600) plus Justice Canada ($1,819) plus FHQ capital ($53,681) = approximately $12,230,898. OSR includes Buffalo Crossing Gas Bar revenue, care home rent, FHQ Tribal Council, First Nations Trust, administration fees, interest, and other revenue.
